summary: Lyceum build paused on two key CEO decisions: Family tab scope and content audience direction.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Good morning team. Quick standup on Lyceum Build Out. We've got 8 tickets in flight across engineering, marketing, and revenue. The board is fairly loaded — Elon's carrying four tickets, Alex has three, Dave has one on market monitoring. Today I want to hear where we stand on the build, what's blocking us, and what needs Joshua's eyes. Let's go around. Elon, start us off. Elon (CTO): We've got three uncommitted hub changes sitting out there — that's ticket A-072. One of those is ready to commit now, the other two need a bit more testing. The Family tab spec on A-042 is progressing but it's already at 455 lines, which tells me scope is creeping. I'd rather ship a tight v1 than let that spec balloon into something we can't execute on. My recommendation: commit what's clean, freeze the Family tab spec, and get Joshua to sign off on the scope before we write another line.

transcript.speaker: Steve Jobs
transcript.message: Elon's right about the Family tab. 455 lines is a spec that's lost its focus. The question isn't what more can we add — it's what can we cut so the experience is unmistakable. A Family tab should do one thing brilliantly on first launch. Everything else is v2. I'd want Joshua to define the single use case that matters most before we touch it again.

transcript.speaker: Chris Vermeullen
transcript.message: From the financial tools side, nothing actionable on Lyceum build specifically. Dave's got the Fear and Greed index at 38 on A-077 — that's cautious territory. No spend implications for the build right now, but worth noting if we're planning any paid launch campaigns. Keep the powder dry until sentiment shifts.

transcript.speaker: Dave
transcript.message: Chris is right, index at 38 means we're in a fear-leaning market. For Lyceum monetization timing, that actually works in our favor — people invest in education and self-improvement tools during uncertainty. Nothing new to add beyond what A-077 is already tracking. I'd rather we nail the product before I spin up any revenue motions.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.message: I'm working through the faith lesson content on A-074 and the devotional content on A-075. Seven lessons drafted, eight faith dimensions mapped. The content engine is building but I need direction from Joshua on tone and audience — are we building for Joshua's personal use first or are we thinking about a broader audience from day one? That changes everything about how I write.

transcript.speaker: James Truchard
transcript.message: From an instrumentation perspective, I'm looking at how we measure engagement once this thing is live. We should be thinking about telemetry now — what signals tell us the Lyceum is actually working for the user? Session depth, lesson completion rates, return frequency. Nothing to ticket yet, but I want the engineering team thinking about measurement hooks during the build, not after.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Good point, James. Let's do a quick cross-talk round. Elon, does James's telemetry ask change anything about the current hub commits? Elon (CTO): Not for the one commit that's ready — that's clean infrastructure work. But James is right, we should bake measurement in early. I'd extend A-042 to include basic telemetry hooks in the Family tab spec rather than bolting it on later. That's a five-line addition, not a scope explosion.

transcript.speaker: Steve Jobs
transcript.message: Agreed, but only if the metrics serve the user experience, not just a dashboard. Measure what matters to the person using it, not what makes us feel busy.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Alright, clean landing. Here's the summary. Priority one: Elon commits the one clean hub change — that's the only approved agent action we have and it's low-hanging fruit. Priority two: we need Joshua to define the Family tab v1 scope so we stop the spec creep on A-042. Priority three: Joshua needs to set the content audience direction so Alex can finalize the lesson and devotional work. James, keep thinking about telemetry — we'll formalize that when the spec is locked. Good standup, team. Let's keep it tight.

actionItems.text: Review and approve Family tab v1 scope to freeze spec on A-042
Decide content audience direction for faith lessons (A-074) and devotionals (A-075)

topPriorities: Commit clean hub change to keep engineering momentum
Get CEO decision on Family tab v1 scope to prevent spec creep
Define content audience direction for faith lessons and devotionals

ceo_questions.question: What is the single primary use case for the Family tab v1?
ceo_questions.context: The spec is at 455 lines and growing — the team wants to freeze scope and ship a focused v1.
options: Full family dashboard with lessons integration

ceo_questions.question: Who is the target audience for Lyceum faith content?
ceo_questions.context: Alex needs to know if the tone and depth should be personal-use or built for a broader audience from the start.
options: Personal use for Joshua first, broader audience later
Build for a general audience from day one
